item.title: BGDCL holds stakeholder meeting on gas distribution
item.description: The Bakhrabad Gas Distribution Company Limited held a stakeholder meeting on gas distribution system and customer service at Homna in Cumilla on Tuesday......

item.title: CU approves Tk 503cr budget for FY27
item.description: The finance committee and syndicate of Chittagong University on Tuesday approved a revised revenue budget of Tk 489.99 crore for the 2025–26 financial year and a projected budget of Tk 503.47 crore for the FY 2026–27......

item.title: Govt pursuing Hasina extradition: Shama
item.description: State minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam on Wednesday said the government is continuing efforts to extradite former prime minister Sheikh Hasina to Bangladesh, saying the people want her returned to face trial......

item.title: Govt reconstitutes National Drug Advisory Council
item.description: The government has reconstituted the 24-member National Drug Advisory Council under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 2023, with the health and family welfare minister as its chairman......

item.title: Ex-AL MP Nur Mohammad sent to jail
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday sent former Awami League lawmaker from Jamalpur-1 constituency Md Nur Mohammad to jail after his three-day remand ended in a case filed under the Anti-Terrorism Act......

item.title: Youth gets life term for raping schoolgirl in Kushtia
item.description: A Kushtia court on Wednesday sentenced a 32-year-old man to life imprisonment for raping a schoolgirl in the district in 2018......

item.title: Ahmed Sofa’s birth anniv observed
item.description: The 83rd anniversary of birth of poet, novelist, essayist and thinker Ahmed Sofa was observed on Wednesday......

item.title: PM reviews projects to ease Dhaka traffic
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday reviewed the progress of major infrastructure projects aimed at easing traffic congestion in the capital, including the development of a circular road network and better use of surrounding waterways......

item.title: June rainfall 29pc below normal
item.description: The country received 29.4 per cent less rainfall than normal in June, the first month of the monsoon season, with all divisions except Mymensingh and Sylhet recording below-average precipitation, according to the Bangladesh Meteorological Department......

item.title: Trump upbeat as US, Iran hold indirect talks in Qatar
item.description: US president Donald Trump hailed the progress on Wednesday of indirect talks between the United States and Iran in Qatar, as the sides aimed to push forward negotiations and quell tensions following exchanges of fire......

item.title: Troops to stay in Lebanon, Syria, Gaza: Israel
item.description: Israel’s defence minister said Wednesday that Israeli forces would remain in so-called security zones established in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza ‘until further notice’......

item.title: US stars relish primetime moment against Bosnia
item.description: Soccer has made astounding progress in America in recent years, but the United States players know the spotlight on Wednesday’s knockout clash against Bosnia and Herzegovina will be by far the brightest yet......

item.title: 53pc teacher vacancies hit CHT edn hard
item.description: A Class X science student, Borna Shree Tanchangya, travels nearly two kilometres from her hill-perched home every weekday to attend class at Rowangchari Government High School in Bandarban......

item.title: Jamaat slams govt for not taking July programmes
item.description: Leaders of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Wednesday criticised the government for not announcing any programme to observe the second anniversary of the July Uprising......

item.title: Kane rescues England from DR Congo calamity
item.description:
Harry Kane rescued England from a seismic World Cup shock with two late goals to beat the Democratic Republic of Congo 2-1 in Atlanta and secure a place in the last 16.
...

item.title: Govt should abandon AL-era denial strategy
item.description: CONTRARY to the home affairs minister’s claim that crime has declined compared with that in 15 years, rights organisations continue to report incidents of mob violence and a rise in rape cases. Manbadhikar Sangskriti Foundation’s monthly report records 78 incidents of mob violence in June, resulting in 33 deaths and 126 serious injuries, while 32 were killed and 71 were......

item.title: Violence against public officials mustn’t go unpunished
item.description: The attack on at least 16 forest officials during an eviction drive at Sreepur in Gazipur on June 30 once again shows the risks that government officials tasked with protecting public land from illegal occupation face. According to the forest department, the officials were attacked when they tried to remove unauthorised structures erected on about an acre of forest land at......

item.title: Bangladesh, Malaysia and test of real partnership
item.description: IN AN Indo-Pacific increasingly narrated through the rivalry of giants, the diplomacy that may matter most is often the quietest kind: the lateral ties that middle and emerging powers build with one another, away from the gravitational pull of Washington and Beijing. These partnerships rarely make headlines, yet they are where much of Asia’s economic future......

item.title: Reaping dividend of educated women
item.description: THE enrolment rate of women in higher education has increased sharply in recent years, which is a commendable achievement, resulting in socioeconomic advancement. According to the University Grants Commission’s latest annual report, women accounted for nearly half of all students in public universities in 2023, with men making up 53 percent of enrolment and......

item.title: From exporting labour to exporting human capital
item.description: FEW countries have transformed themselves as dramatically as Bangladesh since independence in 1971. Once derided by foreign observers as a ‘basket case,’ Bangladesh has emerged as South Asia’s second-largest economy and one of the developing world’s most compelling success stories......

item.title: Can anyone stop France at the World Cup?
item.description: The first 48-team World Cup will soon be whittled down to the last 16, and already it looks like a question of whether anybody can stop a flying France side....

item.title: 'Iran, Iran!' Iranian World Cup squad serenaded on return home
item.description: Iran's disappointed World Cup squad returned to a much warmer welcome at Tehran airport on Wednesday than they experienced on arrival in the US for their three group games....

item.title: England refused to accept defeat in 'beautiful' DR Congo win: Tuchel
item.description:
Thomas Tuchel said England had defied the ghosts of previous major tournament disappointments by coming from behind to avoid a shock World Cup elimination at the hands of the Democratic Republic of Congo.
...

item.title: 109 more trafficking victims return from Cambodia
item.description: Another 109 Bangladeshi nationals, who were reportedly trafficked to Cambodia, returned home early Wednesday, bringing the total number of rescued victims repatriated over the past four days to 362......

item.title: HC seeks report on action against burnt engine oil stoves
item.description: The High Court on Wednesday asked the government to explain why its alleged failure to monitor, regulate and completely prohibit the manufacture, commercial sale, online promotion and widespread use of hazardous waste motor oil stoves, widely known as ‘pora mobil chula’, should not be declared illegal......

item.title: DU marks 105th founding anniv
item.description: Students, teachers and employees of the University of Dhaka on Wednesday celebrated the institution’s 105th founding anniversary with rallies, discussions, cultural programmes and other events......

item.title: JnU approves budget without research allocation
item.description: Jagannath University has approved a Tk 330 crore budget for the 2026-27 financial year without any allocation for research, following a similar move by Dhaka University......

item.title: Opposition to push constitutional reform campaign: Jamaat
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman, also opposition leader in the Jatiya Sangsad, on Wednesday said that his party would continue its campaign to implement the public mandate for constitutional reform, arguing that the government had failed to honour the outcome of the referendum held alongside the JS polls......

item.title: Police recover shotgun at Bosila house
item.description: The police recovered a shotgun from the false ceiling of a bathroom in an under-construction building at Bosila in Dhaka early Wednesday......

item.title: NU waives exam fees for July uprising injured students
item.description: The National University in a press release on Wednesday said that the NU had waived examination-related fees for students in its affiliated colleges who were injured during the July–August 2024 student-led mass uprising......

item.title: 4 decades of contemporary art on display
item.description: A group art exhibition featuring works by 15 artists from last four decades is under way at the Quamrul Hassan Exhibition Hall of Bengal Shilpalay in Dhanmondi OF Dhaka......

item.title: Dry Pharmacology Lab inaugurated at BMU
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University inaugurated the Dry Pharmacology Lab at the pharmacology department of the university in Dhaka on Wednesday......
